problem_statement: Let $T=688$. Let $a$ be the least nonzero digit in $T$, and let $b$ be the greatest digit in $T$. In square $N O R M, N O=b$, and points $P_{1}$ and $P_{2}$ lie on $\overline{N O}$ and $\overline{O R}$, respectively, so that $O P_{1}=O P_{2}=a$. A circle centered at $O$ has radius $a$, and quarter-circular arc $\widehat{P_{1} P_{2}}$ is drawn. There is a circle that is tangent to $\widehat{P_{1} P_{2}}$ and to sides $\overline{M N}$ and $\overline{M R}$. The radius of this circle can be written in the form $x-y \sqrt{2}$, where $x$ and $y$ are positive integers. Compute $x+y$.
